untranslatable_reason: BAB (Betting Against Beta) is a factor-based portfolio construction strategy that requires beta coefficient calculation for each security relative to market benchmark. Beta is not available in the allowed indicators list (which contains only technical indicators: price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, Volume, Z-score, and bar properties). The signal requires: (1) ranking securities by ex-ante beta, (2) identifying bottom/top decile securities by beta, (3) constructing market-neutral leverage adjustments based on beta spreads. None of these operations can be performed with the available technical indicators. This requires a factor data library or fundamental/statistical analysis module outside the scope of technical indicators.
translation_notes: Signal describes a quantitative factor strategy requiring beta calculations and cross-sectional security ranking. The entry/exit conditions depend on beta coefficient estimates and portfolio rebalancing triggers that cannot be derived from standard technical indicators alone. A proper implementation would require: (a) a beta estimation module, (b) cross-sectional ranking capability, (c) leverage/deleveraging logic tied to correlation/covariance matrices, and (d) market impact/funding liquidity monitoring — none of which map to the available indicator set.
